$comment: Divergence desk: real-history vs career-universe stories. The historical fact rides the {rival} token (the displaced winner/champion) and is always attributed as the real record; the career outcome is always framed as this universe's alternate timeline - never as a change to real history.

templates.headline: Alternate timeline: {winner} wins where history chose {rival}
templates.summary: In this universe, {winner} won round {round}; the historical record shows {rival}.

sections.lead: The record books and this championship have parted company[[?venue: at {venue}]]: in the real {year}, {rival} took this race. In the player's universe, the winner reads {winner}[[?winnerTeam: , for {winnerTeam}]].
sections.context: The Archive Desk keeps both columns side by side - the historical record stands untouched; this timeline simply wrote a different line.
sections.close: One changed result rarely stays contained. The desk will be watching where this timeline goes next.

templates.headline: A different champion: this universe crowns {winner}
templates.deck: The {year} title diverges from the historical record
templates.summary: This universe's {year} champion is {winner}; history crowned {rival}.

sections.lead: The biggest divergence a season can produce: the {year} championship of this universe belongs to {winner}, where the historical record crowned {rival}.
sections.context: Every what-if argument in the sport starts exactly here - one season, two verdicts. The real {year} is unchanged in the archive; this timeline now carries its own.
sections.close: The Archive Desk files both champions, clearly labeled, one universe apart.

templates.headline: History holds: {winner} champion in both timelines
templates.deck: The {year} season converges with the record
templates.summary: This universe's {year} season crowned the same champion as history: {winner}.

sections.lead: For all the changed weekends along the way, the {year} season arrived at the same verdict as the record books: {winner}, champion in both timelines.
sections.close: Some outcomes, it seems, were simply meant.
